parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Describe the steps to construct the perpendicular bisector of a line segment PQ.
parts.correctAnswer: Open compass > half PQ; arcs from P and Q; join intersections.

markScheme.desc: Set compass to more than half PQ; draw arcs from P and from Q
Join the two arc intersections to give the perpendicular bisector

workingSteps: 1. Open compass to radius greater than half the length of PQ.
2. Draw arcs above and below PQ from centre P, then from centre Q.
3. Join the two intersection points of the arcs — this is the perpendicular bisector.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
The perpendicular bisector of PQ passes through all points that are equidistant from P and Q.
Describe the locus of points equidistant from two fixed points A and B.
workingSteps: The locus is the perpendicular bisector of the line segment AB.
It passes through the midpoint of AB at right angles to AB.

questions.context: A region R is described as: more than 3 cm from a fixed point P, and closer to point A than to point B.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Describe the boundary for 'more than 3 cm from P'.
parts.correctAnswer: A circle with centre P and radius 3 cm; the region is outside this circle.

markScheme.desc: Circle with centre P, radius 3 cm
Region R is outside this circle

workingSteps: Points exactly 3 cm from P form a circle (centre P, radius 3 cm).
More than 3 cm from P means outside this circle.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Describe the boundary for 'closer to A than to B'.
parts.correctAnswer: The perpendicular bisector of AB; closer to A is the half-plane containing A.

markScheme.desc: Perpendicular bisector of AB is the boundary
Region is the half-plane on the same side as A

workingSteps: Points equidistant from A and B lie on the perpendicular bisector of AB.
Closer to A than B means on the A-side of this bisector.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Give a reason why you would use a compass rather than a protractor to construct a perpendicular bisector.
parts.correctAnswer: A compass gives exact equal distances from both endpoints without measuring angles.

markScheme.desc: Compass maintains equal distance from each endpoint
Avoids measuring error / more accurate construction oe

workingSteps: A compass ensures that arcs are drawn at exactly equal radii from each endpoint.
This guarantees the construction is accurate without needing to measure angles.
